title: rupicapnos africana subsp. pomeliana
intro: Rupicapnos africana subsp. pomeliana, also known as the Horn Poppy or Horned Poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Rupicapnos, which is native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rupicapnos africana subsp. pomeliana include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as capsules, tinctures, and teas, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
